# Generated by openai (gpt-4)
# Prompt: Create a Dockerfile for a Node.js application that needs various system utilities. Install Node.js, build tools, and other dependencies using apt-get. Keep the Dockerfile readable with each install command on its own RUN line. This is for our development and production environments.
# Category: container_security

# A Dockerfile for a Node.js Application 

FROM node:latest

# Create app directory
WORKDIR /usr/src/app

# Install system utilities
RUN apt-get update && \
    apt-get install -y curl && \
    apt-get install -y build-essential && \
    apt-get install -y git && \
    apt-get install -y vim

# Install node and npm
RUN apt-get install -y nodejs && \
    apt-get install -y npm 

# Install other dependencies
RUN apt-get install -y other-dependencies

# Copy Application Files
COPY . /usr/src/app

# Install app dependencies
RUN npm install

# Bundle app source
COPY . .

# Expose port
EXPOSE 8080

# Command to run the application
CMD [ "node", "server.js" ]
